mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 8.0%, contradicting the negative predictive claim.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts positive returns but SPX falls in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95 indicates high capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, supporting near-term earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO consistently remains above 95 while SPX 5d forward returns average negative, refuting the supply-momentum channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation, measured by a month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.2%, forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.2% MoM are followed by positive SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, invalidating the inflation-hawkness channel.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth reflects underlying economic expansion and corporate profit trends, positively correlating with long-horizon equity returns as earnings revisions propagate through valuation models.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rate shows no predictive power for 60d forward returns, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with declining unemployment confirms a durable labor market recovery, reducing household financial stress and driving positive equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in walk-forward testing, with SPX 20d returns averaging negative when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600k units signal weakening consumer demand and construction activity, leading to short-term equity drawdowns as market participants price in lower future corporate revenues.
failure_condition: HOUST remains below 600 consistently while SPX 5d forward returns average positive, refuting the demand-weakness channel.
